fields.description:
## Why
Live red-error before Save + per-kind help. Validated by spike E4 (server round-trip p99 ~0.5ms,
single source of truth, no client-side ajv).

## What
- A `/validate` editor endpoint (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py`) hitting the existing Python
  registry validator; the front-end (`editor_assets/`) debounces edits and maps returned errors to
  editor markers (red error before Save). The validation applies to the ACTIVE config representation
  and CARRIES OVER to S8's structured per-field entries (S8 replaces the raw textarea but the same
  validation drives the per-field errors) — sequence S7 before S8.
- ENDPOINT/VALIDATOR FAILURE surfacing: a 500/timeout/validator-crash surfaces a distinct, visible
  "validation unavailable" state (NOT a false "valid", NOT a silent swallow), so the author is never
  misled into Saving against a dead validator.
- A per-kind HELP panel rendering element types + expected JSON shape, driven by the contracts/descriptions.

## Scope
In: validate endpoint + debounced markers + endpoint-failure state + help panel. Out: structured fields (S8).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Editing the step config shows a red error before Save (debounced server round-trip); a valid config clears it.
- [ ] An endpoint/validator failure (500/timeout) surfaces a visible "validation unavailable" state, never a false-valid or silent swallow.
- [ ] The help panel shows element types + the expected JSON shape per kind.
- [ ] A browser e2e exercises live validation (invalid -> error, fix -> clear) and the unavailable state.
- [ ] `/validate` returns a DEFINED response shape `{ok: bool, errors: [{path, message}]}`; Save is BLOCKED while validation errors exist OR while validation is in the 'unavailable' state (fail-closed Save).
- [ ] A test covers malformed/unparseable JSON and empty config input (distinct from a schema-invalid-but-parseable config).
